steps.title: Find the specific obstacles Text 1 identifies
steps.content: Text 1 claims dating is unreliable at Sterkfontein because of two specific problems: mixed soil layers prevent stratigraphic dating, and animal bones may be relocated by flooding. These are the obstacles blocking reliable age assignment.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's solution to these obstacles
steps.content: Choice C says the researchers would argue their technique is better suited than other methods to Sterkfontein's unique challenges—and that's exactly what Text 2 demonstrates. Cosmogenic nuclide dating analyzes the breccia surrounding the fossils, explicitly avoiding both the problematic soil-layer and animal-bone approaches. The other choices either introduce unsupported claims (corruption in choice A), contradict the solution (choice B suggests combining the old methods rather than replacing them), or misstate the advantage (choice D claims it works because applied “directly” rather than because it avoids the flawed methods). That matches choice C — C is correct.
